user_input: How does the carbon footprint of the Apple Mac mini compare to other models?
retrieved_contexts: ['October 29, 2024 - According to Apple, these changes have cut the carbon footprint of the Mac by more than 80 percent, and to make up the remaining emissions, Apple "applies high-quality carbon credits from nature based products" such as those from the Restore Fund.', 'November 10, 2020 - and reduced the energy needed to use the device, driving down the overall Mac mini carbon · footprint by 34 percent compared with the previous generation.4 Apple is committed to using · carbon life cycle assessments to identify opportunities to drive down product greenhouse gas ·', 'Carbon reductions are calculated against a business-as-usual baseline scenario: No use of clean electricity for manufacturing or product use, beyond what is already available on the latest modeled grid; Apple’s carbon intensity of key materials as of 2015; and Apple’s average mix of transportation modes by product line across three years. Learn more at apple.com/2030. Results are compared to previous-generation 3.2GHz 6-core Intel Core i7-based Mac mini systems with Intel Iris UHD Graphics 630, 64GB of RAM, and 2TB SSD. Published February 25, 2026']
response: According to the provided context, the carbon footprint of the Apple Mac mini has been reduced by 34 percent compared to the previous generation, as of November 10, 2020. However, there is no information provided about how the carbon footprint of the Mac mini compares to other models, such as the latest or other specific models.
